name: Isis
shortDescription: Isis is an ancient Egyptian goddess of magic, motherhood, and healing, revered as a powerful protector and a symbol of feminine power. In Tarot, she embodies intuition, hidden knowledge, and the mysteries of the unconscious.
description: Isis, derived from the Egyptian ꜣst (conventionally vocalized as Aset), is a major goddess in ancient Egyptian religion whose worship spread throughout the Greco-Roman world. She was first worshiped in ancient Egypt during the Early Dynastic Period (c. 3100–c. 2686 BCE), and her cult lasted well into the Greco-Roman period. Isis was revered as the ideal mother and wife, patron of nature and magic. She was a friend to slaves, sinners, artisans, and the downtrodden, but she also listened to the prayers of the wealthy, maidens, aristocrats, and rulers. Isis is often depicted as a woman wearing a sheath dress and either the hieroglyphic sign for a throne on her head, or the solar disk and horns. As a mother, she is often shown nursing her son Horus. Her most famous myth revolves around her husband and brother Osiris, whom his brother Set murdered. Isis searched for Osiris's body, reassembled it, and resurrected him, conceiving their son Horus through magical means. In Tarot, Isis represents the High Priestess archetype, embodying intuition, hidden knowledge, and the mysteries of the unconscious. She symbolizes the divine feminine, inner wisdom, and the power of understanding beyond the material realm. She is associated with the veil between worlds and accessing the secrets of the soul. Her presence in a reading suggests a need to trust one's intuition and delve into the unseen aspects of a situation.

sources.title: Egyptian Ideas of the Future Life
sources.author: E. A. Wallis Budge
